title: Thread-safety model in lichen_l2_enable() is well-reasoned
description: lichen_l2.c:247-314: The shutdown sequence (clear callback -> signal thread -> release mutex -> join -> reacquire mutexes -> cleanup) correctly handles the race between in-flight callbacks and cleanup. The comment at lines 279-288 explaining the ordering is accurate. Worth defending if someone proposes 'simplifying' by removing the double-mutex acquisition or moving cleanup earlier.
close_reason: P3 observations acknowledged, no fix needed
